member.name: T:ElementIT.PowUpload.License
member.summary: Static class provides access to a PowUpload component license.
member.remarks: Provides details of a component license type, a serial key and a number of days remaining. This class allows to set or get serial key and get number of days remaining.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.License.LicenseType
member.summary: Gets a string representation of the component license state.
member.value: Component license state string.
member.remarks:
Returns "Full License" with the serial key number if the correct key has set in serialKey attribute of the Web.config file or in the SerialKey property.
Returns "Free for personal use only" in other cases.
Examples:
Free for personal use only
Full License. Serial key: xxxxxxxxxxxx

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.License.SerialKey
member.summary: Gets or sets the serial key of the PowUpload component.
member.value: A string representing the serial key.
member.remarks:
This property must be set before any use of the component. It can be done at the .aspx page with the POST Form or at the Global.asax file.
Use this proprty to set the serial key in code. It can be usefull if you can't or don't wish to set the key in the serialKey attribute of the Web.config file.

member.name: T:ElementIT.PowUpload.Progress
member.summary: Allows to monitor the progress of the files upload.

member.name: M:ElementIT.PowUpload.Progress.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the Progress object with specified ID.
member.param: A string representing ID of the corresponding upload process.
member.remarks: The ID parameter is used to bind the Progress object with the upload process.

member.name: M:ElementIT.PowUpload.Progress.CancelUpload(System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Stops upload, terminates the connection with the client if demanded and deletes all files uploaded if demanded.
member.param: Set to true if request should be terminated; otherwise, false.
Set to true if the uploaded files should be deleted; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:ElementIT.PowUpload.Progress.CancelUpload(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Stops upload and if specified terminates the connection with the client.
member.param: Set to true if request should be terminated; otherwise, false.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.Progress.id
member.summary: Gets or sets ID to bind the Progress object with the upload process.
member.value: The ID of the Progress object instanse.
member.remarks: The same as ID parameter passed to constructor.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.Progress.BytesPerSecondAverage
member.summary: Gets the average data transfer rate for the upload in bytes per second.
member.value: The average data transfer rate in bytes per second.
member.remarks: Value determined by the following formula: UploadedBytes/SecondsDone

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.Progress.BytesPerSecondCurrent
member.summary: Gets the current data transfer rate for the upload in bytes per second.
member.value: The current data transfer rate in bytes per second.
member.remarks:
Returns transfer rate for the last data chunk uploaded.
This value varies a lot depending on the server load degree and the Internet connection rate. This is the current transfer rate. Use BytesPerSecondAverage property to get an average transfer rate.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.Progress.SecondsLeftCurrent
member.summary: Gets the estimated number of seconds left to complete upload.
member.value: The estimated number of seconds left.
member.remarks:
This value is based on the current transfer rate. So it varies a lot with time.
Use SecondsLeftAverage property to get more probable number of seconds left.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.Progress.SecondsLeftAverage
member.summary: Gets the estimated number of seconds left to complete upload.
member.value: The estimated number of seconds left.
member.remarks: This value is based on the average transfer rate. This value is more stable than SecondsLeftCurrent property.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.Progress.SecondsDone
member.summary: Gets the number of seconds elapsed since the upload start.
member.value: The number of seconds elapsed since the upload start.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.Progress.PercentDone
member.summary: Gets the percentage of the upload transfer is completed.
member.value: Percentage of the upload process is completed.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.Progress.TotalBytes
member.summary: Gets the total size of the HTTP POST request.
member.value: Total size of the HTTP POST request in bytes.
member.remarks: HTTP POST request includes files content, files names and other ancillary data. So HTTP POST request size is greater than total files size.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.Progress.UploadStatus
member.summary: Gets the current upload status.
member.value: The UploadState of the upload.
member.remarks: See UploadState for details.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.Progress.UploadedFilesCount
member.summary: Gets completely uploaded files count.
member.value: The number of completely uploaded files.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.Progress.CurrentFileName
member.summary: Gets the name of the currently uploading file.
member.value: A string that represents name of the currently uploading file.
member.remarks: Returns path of the file on the client's computer.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.Progress.CurrentFileUploadedBytes
member.summary: Gets the number of bytes of the currently uploading file that have been received by the server.
member.value: The number of bytes of the currently uploading file that have been received by the server.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.Progress.UploadComplete
member.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether data transfer is complete.
member.value: true if data transfer has finished; otherwise, false.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.Progress.UploadedBytes
member.summary: Gets the number of bytes that have been received by the server.
member.value: The number of bytes that have been received by the server.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.Progress.UploadStatusString
member.summary: Returns a string representation of the current upload status.
member.value: A string that represents status of the upload.
member.remarks: Use this property to show upload status for user.

example.code:
Progress progr = new Progress(Request.QueryString["uploadID"]);
Response.Write (“Upload status: ” + progr.UploadStatusString);

member.name: T:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadState
member.summary: Specifies the available Upload process states.

member.name: F:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadState.Waiting
member.summary: Initial state. The Upload process with the same ID as specified in the constructor hasn't started.

member.name: F:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadState.Uploading
member.summary: Upload process is in progress.

member.name: F:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadState.Complete
member.summary: Upload process has completed.

member.name: F:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadState.Error
member.summary: An error occurs while the Upload process.

member.name: F:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadState.Cancelled
member.summary: The Upload process cancelled by the user or by the Progress.CancelUpload method.

member.name: F:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadState.Rejected
member.summary: The Upload process was rejected due to the POST request size exceeds the maximum or minimum allowed value.

member.name: T:ElementIT.PowUpload.Upload
member.summary: Gives access to the files contained in an HTTP POST request.

member.name: M:ElementIT.PowUpload.Upload.IsUploadInitialized
member.summary: Returns a value indicating whether the Upload process with the ID equals to the hash code of the current Request was initialized.
member.returns: true if Upload process was initialized; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:ElementIT.PowUpload.Upload.IsUploadInitialized(System.String)
member.summary: Returns a value indicating whether the Upload process with the specified ID was initialized.
member.param: Unique identifier of the upload process.
member.returns: true if Upload process was initialized; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:ElementIT.PowUpload.Upload.#ctor

overloads.summary: Initializes a new instance of the Upload class.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the Upload class with the ID equals to the hash code of the current Request.
member.remarks:
Multiple users can upload files at the same time. A Session object that can help to distinguish users uploading files is inaccessible. To identify upload processes and to associate them with the corresponding Progress objects unique identifiers (ID) are used. The Upload class has two constructors. They differ in a way the ID is received.
This constructor has no parameters. It uses HashCode of the current Request object as an ID. The Request object received by an Upload module from an user page request is the same as the one in the page there Upload object creating. That is why HashCode is the same.

member.name: M:ElementIT.PowUpload.Upload.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the Upload class with specified ID.
member.param: Unique identifier of the upload process.
member.remarks:
Multiple users can upload files at the same time. A Session object that can help to distinguish users uploading files is inaccessible. To identify upload processes and to associate them with the corresponding Progress objects unique identifiers (ID) are used. The Upload class has two constructors. They differ in a way the ID is received.
This constructor must get an ID. Query string field with ID of the upload process should be passed to the file that processing Form and to the Progress bar page. An "Action" parameter of the Form should be set to a string like "<file name>?UploadID=xxxxx" (for example, Action="processupload.aspx?UploadID=123456789"). The same ID should be added to the Progress bar page name (for example, "progress.aspx?UploadID=123456789").

member.name: M:ElementIT.PowUpload.Upload.Dispose
member.summary: Forces the release of all resources used by this Upload object : Deletes temporary files held by the Upload object, disposes a collection of UploadedFile objects, closes UploadedFile streams created by the InputStream property.
member.remarks: You aren't obliged to call this method. NET framework calls it by the Garbage Collector once the Upload object goes out of scope. Do call this method to immediatly release all resources occupied by the last upload.

member.name: M:ElementIT.PowUpload.Upload.Finalize
member.summary: Executes own Dispose() method to free resources and perform other cleanup operations before the Object is reclaimed by garbage collection.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.Upload.Files
member.summary: Gets the collection of client-uploaded files.
member.value: An UploadedFileCollection object representing a collection of client-uploaded files.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.Upload.LastError
member.summary: Gets the last Exception occurred during the upload process.
member.value: The Exception object that represents the last occurred error.
member.remarks: Errors occurred in processing the files uploaded willn't be returned by this property. This property is useful to determine whether the files was uploaded successfully or an error occurred. The property is null if the upload completed succesfully.

member.name: T:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFile
member.summary: Provides a way to access individual files that have been uploaded by a client.
member.remarks:
The UploadedFileCollection class provides access to all the files uploaded from a client as a file collection. UploadedFile provides properties and methods to get information on an individual file and to read and save the file. Files are uploaded in MIME multipart/form-data format and are stored in temp folder until explicitly moved to needed folder or deleted by Destructor or Dispose method.
You cannot create an UploadedFile object directly. You must obtain it by referencing an item within the Files property of an Upload object.

member.name: M:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFile.CloseInputStream
member.summary: Closes the stream created by the InputStream property and releases all resources (such as file handles) associated with the stream.

member.name: M:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFile.CopyTo(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Copies an uploaded file to a new file. Overwriting a file of the same name is allowed.
member.param: The name of the destination file. This cannot be a directory.
true if the destination file can be overwritten; otherwise, false.
member.remarks:
Use an absolute path at the filename argument. To get the physical path that corresponds to the Web application virtual folder use Server.Mappath(“”).
This method makes a new copy of the uploaded file. If you wish to make just a single copy of the file use the SaveAs method. It moves the file therefore it works faster.

member.name: M:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFile.CopyTo(System.String)
member.summary: Copies an uploaded file to a new file. Overwriting a file of the same name is not allowed.
member.param: The name of the destination file. This cannot be a directory.
member.remarks:
Use an absolute path at the filename argument. To get the physical path that corresponds to the Web application virtual folder use Server.Mappath(“”).
This method makes a new copy of the uploaded file. If you wish to make just a single copy of the file use the SaveAs method. It moves the file therefore it works faster.

member.name: M:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFile.SaveAs(System.String,System.Boolean)

overloads.summary: Moves an uploaded file to a new location, providing the option to specify file name.

member.summary: Moves an uploaded file to a new location, providing the option to specify file name. Overwriting a file of the same name is allowed.
member.param: The name of the destination file. This cannot be a directory.
true if the destination file can be overwritten; otherwise, false.
member.remarks:
Use an absolute path at the filename argument. To get the physical path that corresponds to the Web application virtual folder use Server.Mappath(“”).
This method moves the file uploaded from the temporary folder to the specified location. So you shouldn't run this method twice. Use the CopyTo() method if you wish to make multiple copies of the same file.

member.name: M:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFile.SaveAs(System.String)
member.summary: Moves an uploaded file to a new location, providing the option to specify file name. Overwriting a file of the same name is not allowed.
member.param: The name of the destination file. This cannot be a directory.
member.remarks:
Use an absolute path at the filename argument. To get the physical path that corresponds to the Web application virtual folder use Server.Mappath(“”).
This method moves the file uploaded from the temporary folder to the specified location. So you shouldn't run this method twice. Use the CopyTo() method if you wish to make multiple copies of the same file.

member.name: M:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFile.GetSafeName(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the safe file name and extension of the specified path string.
member.param: The path string from which to obtain the file name and extension.
member.returns: A String consisting of the characters after the last directory character in file path.
member.remarks:
All characters that are illegal in file names on Windows and not safe relative path substrings like "\\", "..\" are deleted.
Use only this method to get a safe file name to save the file on the target machine

member.name: M:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFile.Dispose
member.summary: Forces the release of all resources used by this UploadedFile object : Deletes temporary file held by the object, closes stream created by the InputStream property
member.remarks: You aren't obliged to call this method. NET framework calls it by the Garbage Collector once the UploadedFile object goes out of scope. Do call this method to immediatly release all resources occupied by the object.

member.name: M:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFile.Finalize
member.summary: Executes own Dispose() method to free resources and perform other cleanup operations before the Object is reclaimed by garbage collection.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFile.ContentLength
member.summary: Gets the size in bytes of an uploaded file.
member.value: The length of the file.
member.remarks: Returns zero if the file has no content or a user didn't selected a file to upload in the Form. To determine whether a file was selected or not use the FileName property (an empty string indicates the user didn't selected a file).

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFile.ContentType
member.summary: Gets the MIME content type of a file sent by a client.
member.value: The MIME content type of the uploaded file.
member.remarks: For the files of the same type the MIME content type may be different depending on the browser used by a client. Be careful when using this property to define the type of the file.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFile.FieldName
member.summary: Gets the field name of the file uploaded.
member.value: The field name of the file uploaded.
member.remarks: Field name is a string corresponding to the NAME attribute of an <INPUT TYPE="FILE"> item of the upload Form.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFile.TempFileName
member.summary: Gets the path to the temporary file associated with the file uploaded.
member.value: The path to the temporary file.
member.remarks:
A temporary file is used to store the content of the file uploaded. The temporary file is the same as the target file except for it's name.
To store file on server hard disk is better than loading it into memory as the built-in ASP.NET upload support does.
The Destructor or Dispose method deletes the temporary file.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFile.ClientFilePath
member.summary: Gets full name of the file on the client's computer.
member.value: The name of the client's file.
member.remarks:
Empty string indicates the user didn't selected a file.
Depending on the Operating System and Browser the client use this name may contain characters such as question marks and slashes which are illegal in file names on Windows. Be carefull using this file name to save the file on the target machine as the name may contain unsafe relative path substrings like "\\", "..\" or a platform-specific characters that cannot be specified in path like quote ("), less than (<), greater than (>), pipe (|), backspace (\b)!
Use the SafeFileName property to get a safe file name.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFile.Extension
member.summary: Gets the file name extension (including the ".")
member.value: A String containing the extension of the file.
member.remarks: Return value contains the extension of the specified path (including the "."). If the file was not selected by the user or the path have no extension, the value is an empty string.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFile.ImageWidth
member.summary: Gets the width of the image in pixels if the file uploaded is an image.
member.value: The width of the image in pixels.
member.remarks: Returns 0 if the file uploaded isn't an image.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFile.ImageHeight
member.summary: Gets the height of the image in pixels if the file uploaded is an image.
member.value: The height of the image in pixels.
member.remarks: Returns 0 if the file uploaded isn't an image.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFile.IsImage
member.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether the uploaded file is an image or not.
member.value: true if the uploaded file has a valid image format and if GDI+ supports the pixel format of the file; otherwise, false.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFile.FileName
member.summary: Gets full name of the file on the client's computer.
member.value: The name of the client's file.
member.remarks: Returns a value the same as the ClientFilePath property.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFile.SafeFileName
member.summary: Gets a safe file name with an extension.
member.value: A String consisting of the characters after the last directory character in file path.
member.remarks:
All characters that are illegal in file names on Windows and not safe relative path substrings like "\\", "..\" are deleted.
Use only this property to get a safe file name to save the file on the target machine

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFile.InputStream
member.summary: Gets a Stream object which points to an uploaded file in a temporary folder to prepare for reading the content of the file.
member.value: A Stream pointing to an uploaded file.

member.name: T:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFileCollection
member.summary: Provides access to and organizes files uploaded by a client.

remarks.para: You cannot create an UploadedFileCollection object directly. You must obtain it from the Files property of an Upload object.

member.name: M:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFileCollection.GetKey(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns unique key of the file with the specified numerical index in the collection.
member.param: The index of the object name to be returned.
member.value: The field name of the UploadedFile member specified by index.
member.remarks: This method is usefull to determine the field name of the file that was uploaded the first (i.e. with index equals to "0"), etc.

member.name: M:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFileCollection.Dispose
member.summary: Forces the release of all resources used by objects in UploadedFileCollection : Deletes temporary files held by the objects in UploadedFileCollection, closes UploadedFile streams created by the InputStream property.
member.remarks: You aren't obliged to call this method. NET framework calls it by the Garbage Collector once the UploadedFileCollection object goes out of scope. Do call this method to immediatly release all resources occupied by UploadedFileCollection.

member.name: M:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFileCollection.Finalize
member.summary: Executes own Dispose() method to free resources and perform other cleanup operations before the Object is reclaimed by garbage collection.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFileCollection.Item(System.String)
member.summary: Gets an individual UploadedFile object from the file collection by the specified field name.
member.param: The field name of the file corresponding to the NAME attribute of an <INPUT TYPE="FILE"> item of your upload Form.
member.value: The UploadedFile specified by field name.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFileCollection.Item(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets an individual UploadedFile object from the file collection by the specified index.
member.param: The index of the item to get from the file collection.
member.value: The UploadedFile specified by index.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFileCollection.AllKeys
member.summary: Gets a string array containing the keys (field names) of all members in the file collection.
member.value: An array of field names of uploaded files.

member.name: P:ElementIT.PowUpload.UploadedFileCollection.Count
member.summary: Gets the number of UploadedFile objects contained within the UploadedFileCollection instance, which corresponds directly to the number of files found within the HTTP POST request.
member.value: The number of objects contained in the UploadedFileCollection instance.

member.name: T:ElementIT.PowUpload.NamespaceDoc
member.summary:
PowUpload is an ASP.NET control that enables an ASP.NET application to capture and save files uploaded to the web server by a browser. PowUpload is fully compatible with the standard files upload protocol defined in RFC1867 for the HTML POST Form with the <INPUT TYPE=FILE> tags.
PowUpload handles and stores the contents of the POST request to a file on the server hard disk rather than loading it into memory as the built-in ASP.NET upload support does. PowUpload provides rich server-side progress indicator that lets users to monitor the progress of their uploads.
